notes: Research-only BTC 4H trend-following skeleton.
Uses EMA crossover, 200-SMA regime filter, NATR gate, chandelier stop, and opposite-cross exit.
Suggested exposure comes from a vol-target wrapper, but the current report is still unit-notional and pre-cost.
Research-only BTC portfolio construction model.
Base exposure comes from the graduated daily core regime; tactical only tops exposure up to 100% when a live 4H trade is active.
The tactical overlay never overrides a core risk-off state.

This comparison is meant to answer whether BTC should be held, filtered by a slow binary regime sleeve, filtered by a graduated regime sleeve, traded tactically, traded tactically only inside a valid slow regime, or managed through a graduated core plus tactical overlay construction.

The next decision gate is whether graduated exposure closes enough of the gap to passive HODL while still preserving the drawdown-control story that makes managed BTC exposure worth offering, and whether tactical adds anything incremental after that structural improvement.
